hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212), while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will introduce charge complementarity with SSTR2-unique TM6 (272/273/276/279) and disrupt off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current scaffold is capable of strong SSTR2 binding, but off-target interactions at SSTR3/SSTR5 remain a bottleneck. Targeted mutations at non-pharmacophore positions are necessary to improve selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target binding in SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing design on these positions can help disrupt conserved pocket interactions while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).

These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved pocket interactions at SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining the overall peptide scaffold. Positively charged residues at positions 1, 2, 11, and 12 may repel interactions with acidic residues in off-target pockets.
